subject: ap-world-history
title: Africa — Society & Politics
stimulus:
Graduation Ceremony at a Christian Theological Seminary in Northern India, 1897

The image shows two British missionaries (second row, center) surrounded by Indian graduates. The seminary (religious school) was founded in 1871 to educate and train Indian missionaries.

parts.prompt:
Identify ONE development during the period 1450 to 1750 that contributed to the activities shown in
the image.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Western missionaries tried to spread Christianity in Africa and Asia.
1 pt: Christianity spread as imperial powers conquered new territories.
1 pt: Christianity transformed some local religious practices and customs.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE reason why imperialist governments often supported activities of the type shown in the
image during the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Christian missionaries directly supported the “civilizing” or “Christianizing” mission that the imperial powers saw as a rationale for colonization.
1 pt: Christian missionaries often brought supplies and gave aid to local communities, which both legitimized imperialism and lessened government costs.
1 pt: By spreading Christian beliefs, European Christian missionaries often legitimized imperialism.
1 pt: Imperial states often needed missionary schools to provide Western education for local populations in order to serve in colonial administrations.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE way indigenous peoples in Africa and/or Asia resisted the spread of the cultural
practices reflected in the image during the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
3
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Some Asian governments tried to prevent or limit Christian missionaries from entering their countries, and in some cases expelled Christian missionaries who were already in the countries.
1 pt: Some African cultures maintained traditional religious beliefs by blending older practices with Western Christianity.

1 pt: In both Africa and Asia, popular uprisings targeted Christian missionaries in an effort to force them out of the country; for example, during the Boxer Rebellion in China, the 1857 Rebellion in India, or the Cattle Killing Movement in South Africa.
